description:
# Open question

Pre/post hook rollback semantics in detail.

The §8 lifecycle says:
- Pre-invoke fails → rollback prior pre-invokes in reverse
- Invoke fails → rollback prior invokes AND prior pre-invokes in reverse

Open questions:
- The plugin gets ONE `rollback` verb. Payload includes `rolling_back: pre-invoke|invoke` so it knows which side. Plugin tracks its own state between pre and post calls (how — env var, scratch dir under `plugins/<name>/`?).
- If a plugin implements pre-invoke but not invoke, does its pre-invoke side-effect get rolled back on a later plugin's invoke failure?
- Idempotency requirement on rollback: balls won't retry, but balls also can't guarantee rollback was successful. Document the contract: best-effort, exit code ignored, plugin logs anything that mattered to stderr.
- Sample reference: a plugin that needs to remember pre-invoke state across to invoke. Provide a scratch dir convention.
title: Topic: pre/post hook rollback semantics
